21.correct: The minimum age to contest a Lok Sabha election is 25 years.

wrong.A: 18 years is the minimum voting age, not the age to contest.
wrong.B: 21 years is not the qualifying age for the Lok Sabha.
wrong.D: 30 years is the minimum age to become a member of the Rajya Sabha.

21.fact: For the Rajya Sabha the minimum age is 30; for the Lok Sabha and Legislative Assembly it is 25.

22.correct: Among the options, Puducherry is the Union Territory that has a Legislative Assembly (and a Council of Ministers).

wrong.B: Andaman and Nicobar Islands have no assembly.

22.fact: Delhi (NCT) and Puducherry are the UTs with their own legislative assemblies.

23.correct: The Vice-President of India can resign by submitting the resignation letter to the President.

wrong.B: The resignation is not addressed to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.
wrong.C: It is not given to the Prime Minister.
wrong.D: It is not given to the Chief Justice of India.

23.fact: The Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

24.correct: Amrita Pritam's autobiography is titled 'Rasidi Ticket' (Revenue Stamp).

wrong.A: 'Pinjar' is her famous novel, not the autobiography.
wrong.C: 'Main Tenu Phir Milangi' is a famous poem by her.
wrong.D: 'Sunehade' is a poetry collection that won her the Sahitya Akademi Award.

24.fact: Amrita Pritam was the first prominent woman poet of Punjabi literature.

25.correct: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) is the Indian agency responsible for real-time Air Quality Index (AQI) monitoring in cities.

wrong.A: NITI Aayog is a policy body, not the pollution monitor.
wrong.C: 'State reference organisation' is not the responsible national agency.

25.fact: The CPCB operates under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.

26.correct: An Air Quality Early Warning System is primarily designed to alert citizens in advance about upcoming days of high pollution.

wrong.A: It does not aim to reduce soil pollution.
wrong.B: It does not monitor water pollution.
wrong.D: It does not improve industrial air filtration directly.

26.fact: Delhi has such an early-warning system that forecasts air quality several days ahead.

27.correct: The first edition of the Indian Badminton League (the forerunner of the Premier Badminton League) was held in 2013.

wrong.B: 2014 is not the first edition's year.
wrong.D: 2016 is when it was relaunched as the Premier Badminton League, not the first IBL.

27.fact: It was rebranded as the Premier Badminton League (PBL) from 2016.

28.correct: Olfactory receptors are responsible for detecting smell in humans, located in the lining of the nasal cavity.

wrong.D: Visual receptors (in the retina) detect light.

28.fact: Olfactory (smell) and gustatory (taste) senses work together to give us flavour.

29.correct: The Arthashastra, traditionally attributed to Chanakya (Kautilya), deals mainly with statecraft, economics and administration.

wrong.A: It is not a work of poetry and literature.
wrong.B: It is not about religious rituals.
wrong.D: It is not a treatise on astronomy.

29.fact: Chanakya was the mentor of Chandragupta Maurya, founder of the Mauryan Empire.

30.correct: The first steel plants of independent India (Bhilai, Rourkela and Durgapur) aimed to achieve self-reliance in steel and heavy industry.

wrong.A: They had nothing to do with dairy production.
wrong.B: They were for domestic capacity, not primarily export promotion.
wrong.D: Rural employment was not their main objective.

30.fact: These plants were set up during the Second Five-Year Plan with foreign technical collaboration (USSR, Germany, UK).

31.correct: The Iqta system under the Delhi Sultanate resembled European feudalism mainly because both granted revenue rights (over land) in return for military service.

wrong.A: The Iqta was not originally hereditary, unlike much European feudal land.
wrong.D: They were not merely taxation systems on merchants.

31.fact: An 'iqtadar' (or muqti) collected revenue from his iqta and maintained troops for the Sultan.

32.correct: The Indian Councils Act, 1909 (Morley-Minto Reforms) gave Indians a measure of representation in the central legislature for the first time.

wrong.A: The 1861 Act allowed only nominated non-officials.
wrong.B: The 1892 Act expanded councils but is not credited with this representation milestone.

32.fact: The 1909 Act also introduced separate electorates for Muslims, a controversial step.

33.correct: The 5th anniversary of the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan was observed in August 2025.

wrong.A: March is not the anniversary month.
wrong.C: June is not the month observed.

33.fact: The Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan (Drug-Free India Campaign) was launched in August 2020.

34.correct: The Kanwar Yatra, a major pilgrimage of Uttarakhand and northern India, is associated chiefly with the worship of Lord Shiva.

wrong.B: It is not dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi.
wrong.C: It is not dedicated to Ganesha.
wrong.D: It is not dedicated to Goddess Durga.

34.fact: Kanwariyas carry holy Ganga water (often from Haridwar/Gangotri) to offer at Shiva temples during the month of Shravan.

35.correct: In June 2025 a new species of flowering plant named 'Portulaca bharatensis' was discovered in the Aravalli Hills region.

wrong.B: It was not found in the Western Ghats.
wrong.C: It was not discovered in the Eastern Ghats.
wrong.D: It was not found in the Thar Desert.

35.fact: New plant species are regularly described from India's varied ecological regions.

36.correct: When ultrasonic (ultrasound) waves are passed through a cleaning solution, the vibration shakes loose dust, grease and dirt particles, which separate and fall away from the object.
36.fact: Ultrasonic cleaning works through 'cavitation' - tiny bubbles that scrub surfaces clean.

37.correct: According to Census 2011, the Jain community (I) is the most urbanised religious group in India, largely concentrated in urban areas; Christians (II) are not predominantly urban.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong; Christians are not the most urban-concentrated group.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong because Christians are not predominantly urban.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since Jains are highly urban.

37.fact: Jains have among the highest literacy rates of any community in India.

38.correct: Under a fixed exchange-rate system, when the government officially raises the exchange rate so that the domestic currency becomes cheaper, it is called devaluation.

wrong.A: Depreciation is a fall in a currency's value under a floating system, not a deliberate official act under a fixed system.
wrong.B: Appreciation is a rise in currency value.
wrong.D: Revaluation is an official increase in the currency's value (the opposite of devaluation).

38.fact: India devalued the rupee in 1966 and again sharply in 1991.

39.correct: The 'Towards 1000' strategy is associated with the International Solar Alliance (ISA).

wrong.A: The WTO deals with trade rules, not this solar strategy.
wrong.B: The International Court of Justice settles legal disputes.
wrong.D: SAARC is a regional grouping, not linked to this strategy.

39.fact: The ISA, co-founded by India and France, promotes solar energy across sunshine-rich countries.

40.correct: India won the bronze medal in men's hockey at the Paris 2024 Olympics, beating Spain in the bronze-medal match.

wrong.A: The Netherlands won the silver medal.
wrong.C: France did not win the hockey bronze.

40.fact: It was India's second consecutive Olympic hockey bronze, after Tokyo 2020.
